ds-001.analysis: The Canon AE-1 in excellent condition with the 50mm f/1.8 lens typically sells for $250-$350. At $175 with replaced light seals and original accessories, this is well below market value. The seller has excellent reputation.
risks: Film photography has ongoing costs for film and development
No returns on vintage camera sales typical
negotiationTips: At $175, this is already priced well below market. Offer $160 if you want to try, but risk losing it.

ds-002.analysis: An iPhone 14 Pro Max with a scratched screen, low battery health (87%), no original box, and from a new seller with only 67% positive feedback at $1,099 is overpriced. A refurbished unit with warranty from a reputable seller can be found for $850-$950.
risks: New seller with limited feedback history
Scratched screen may worsen over time
No original box reduces resale value
Low battery health may require $99 replacement soon
negotiationTips: Fair price considering condition is around $750-$800
Ask for additional photos of screen scratch
Consider buying Apple refurbished with full warranty instead

ds-003.analysis: A solid walnut 6ft dining table for $400 is a good price. Comparable tables sell for $800-$1500. The water marks are cosmetic and refinishing is straightforward. The seller has strong positive feedback history.
risks: Local pickup only in Portland - must arrange transportation
Water marks may require professional refinishing ($100-$200)
Solid walnut is heavy - need truck or trailer for pickup
negotiationTips: Offer $350 citing refinishing cost for water marks
Ask for detailed photos of water marks to assess refinishing difficulty
Confirm table dimensions fit your space before pickup
